steps.title: Apply the triangle inequality
steps.content: The triangle inequality theorem requires the sum of any two sides to be greater than the third side. With two sides of length 7 and 10, and unknown third side $x$, set up three inequalities: $7+10\gt x$, $7+x\gt 10$, and $10+x\gt 7$.

steps.title: Solve each inequality
steps.content:
From $7+10\gt x$: $x\lt 17$.

From $7+x\gt 10$: $x\gt 3$.

From $10+x\gt 7$: $x\gt -3$, which is always satisfied for positive $x$.

steps.title: Combine constraints
steps.content: Combining the binding constraints gives $3\lt x\lt 17$.

steps.title: Evaluate the choices
steps.content:
• A ($x\lt 3$): gives only values too small — eliminated.
  • B ($x\gt 17$): gives only values too large — eliminated.
  • C ($3\lt x\lt 17$): matches both constraints exactly. ✓
  • D ($x\lt 3$ or $x\gt 17$): these are the excluded regions, not the valid range — eliminated.
C is correct.
